ddls:
CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_code" TEXT /* Code assigned to the location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location (e.g., Oslo) */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the countries table */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the regions table */,
  "location_type" TEXT /* Type of location (e.g., urban, rural) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_locations_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_locations_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data entry */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location where the data was collected */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of data collection */,
  "n" INTEGER /* Number of cases or occurrences */,
  "granularity_time" TEXT /* Granularity of time (e.g., day, week) */,
  "granularity_geo" TEXT /* Granularity of geography (e.g., county, region) */,
  "border" INTEGER /* Border or boundary information */,
  "age" TEXT /* Age group (e.g., total, 0-19) */,
  "sex" TEXT /* Sex (e.g., total, male, female) */,
  "yrwk" TEXT /* Week and year of data collection (e.g., 2020-08) */,
  "season" TEXT /* Season of data collection (e.g., 2019/2020) */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of data collection */,
  "week" INTEGER /* Week of data collection */,
  "cases" INTEGER /* Number of cases */,
  "pop" INTEGER /* Population of the location */,
  "pr100000" INTEGER /* Rate per 100,000 people */,
  "data_source_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the data sources table */,
  "data_collection_method_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the data collection methods table */,
  "disease_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the diseases table */,
  "symptoms" TEXT /* Symptoms associated with the disease */,
  "transmission_methods" TEXT /* Methods of disease transmission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_season FOREIGN KEY ("season") REFERENCES seasons ("season_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_disease_id FOREIGN KEY ("disease_id") REFERENCES diseases ("disease_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_data_collection_method_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_collection_method_id") REFERENCES data_collection_methods ("method_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_data_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_granularity_time FOREIGN KEY ("granularity_time") REFERENCES time_granularities ("granularity_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_granularity_geo FOREIGN KEY ("granularity_geo") REFERENCES geo_granularities ("granularity_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "demographics" (
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each demographic entry */,
  "age" TEXT /* Age group (e.g., total, 0-19) */,
  "sex" TEXT /* Sex (e.g., total, male, female) */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the demographic group */,
  "ethnicity" TEXT /* Ethnicity of the demographic group */,
  "income_level" TEXT /* Income level of the demographic group */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demographic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "time_granularities" (
  "granularity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each granularity */,
  "granularity_name" TEXT /* Name of the granularity (e.g., day, week) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("granularity_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "geo_granularities" (
  "granularity_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each granularity */,
  "granularity_name" TEXT /* Name of the granularity (e.g., county, region) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("granularity_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "seasons" (
  "season_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each season */,
  "season_name" TEXT /* Name of the season (e.g., 2019/2020) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("season_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "institution_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the institutions table */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user within their institution */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_institution_id FOREIGN KEY ("institution_id") REFERENCES institutions ("institution_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data being versioned */,
  "version_name" TEXT /* Name of the version */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date when the version was created */,
  "version_description" TEXT /* Description of the version changes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_versions_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the source */,
  "source_url" TEXT /* URL of the data source */,
  "source_date" TEXT /* Date the data source was published */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_collection_methods" (
  "method_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each method */,
  "method_name" TEXT /* Name of the method */,
  "method_description" TEXT /* Description of the data collection method */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("method_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "diseases" (
  "disease_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each disease */,
  "disease_name" TEXT /* Name of the disease */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("disease_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "institutions" (
  "institution_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each institution */,
  "institution_name" TEXT /* Name of the institution */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("institution_id")
)
